summary.architecture_style: MediaSession-centered WinUI desktop app with adapterized playback, subtitle presentation, and AI provider seams
summary.future_renderer_status: placeholder contracts present; custom renderer not implemented

layers.name: Process Entry And Composition
layers.responsibility: Launch the app and compose runtime dependencies

layers.name: Shell And Presentation
layers.responsibility: Own WinUI controls, stage coordination, view adapters, and projection application

layers.name: Application Orchestration
layers.responsibility: Coordinate shell flows, subtitle workflows, resume policy, and backend-to-session bridging

layers.name: Session Domain State
layers.responsibility: Store immutable timed media-language state and enforce a single write boundary

layers.name: Playback And Presenter Adapters
layers.responsibility: Bridge the app-facing playback and presenter seams to mpv and WinUI hosting

layers.name: AI Providers And Runtime Bootstrap
layers.responsibility: Resolve provider availability, run caption/translation adapters, and bootstrap external runtimes

components.role: Process entry point that creates and activates the shell window
Manual runtime composition of shell, session, workflow, provider, playback, and presenter dependencies
WinUI shell that builds controls, forwards events, and applies projection state
Owns playlist/load/resume/transport orchestration and caption startup gating
Persists resume state from the normalized playback clock on a periodic cadence
Builds, looks up, updates, and clears persisted playback resume entries
Publishes immutable session snapshots at the shell and workflow boundary
Single write boundary for playback clocks, tracks, transcript lanes, translation lanes, and active subtitle presentation
Projects transport, track, and subtitle read models from MediaSession
Combines workflow-selection state with MediaSession timed state into a compatibility snapshot
Stores non-timed workflow selection state separate from MediaSession
Owns subtitle import/generation/translation policy and writes results through MediaSessionCoordinator
Compatibility facade that exposes projection-based workflow state and delegates commands
Converts session subtitle state and render preferences into a renderer-neutral presentation model
Bridges backend state/events into MediaSessionCoordinator
Normalizes mpv engine state behind IPlaybackBackend and IPlaybackClock
Runs external mpv, maintains named-pipe IPC, and translates mpv properties/events into snapshots and track lists
Bridges playback backend state and video presenter events into immutable view-facing event payloads
Presentation-only wrapper around MpvHostControl
Creates the native child host window, initializes the playback backend, syncs host bounds, and forwards input/fullscreen/shortcut events
Coordinates fullscreen overlay lifecycle, subtitle presenter placement, stage sync, and modal suppression
Compatibility subtitle presenter that renders the projection model in a detached overlay window
Composes provider registries, resolves configured providers, and normalizes persisted model selection
Concrete transcription and translation adapters over low-level services
Delegates runtime installation to concrete installers
Applies standard, borderless, PiP, and fullscreen window modes through AppWindow presenters

dependencies.reason: MainWindow requests fully composed shell dependencies from the composition root
Composition root creates ShellController as the shell orchestration surface
Composition root wires the subtitle workflow graph explicitly
ShellController routes playback commands through IPlaybackBackend
ShellController asks the workflow facade to load subtitles during media load
Workflow controller delegates all behavior commands to SubtitleApplicationService
Workflow controller exposes projection-based compatibility snapshots
Subtitle workflows write transcript, translation, and language state through MediaSessionCoordinator
Subtitle workflows resolve persisted models and provider readiness via provider availability service
Playback backend state, tracks, and clock updates are projected into MediaSession through the coordinator
The backend adapter delegates all real media control and observation to the engine
The shell-side host adapter uses the video presenter for stage attachment and bounds sync
The presenter is a thin wrapper over MpvHostControl
StageCoordinator delegates actual subtitle rendering to ISubtitlePresenter
MainWindow applies immutable shell projections to controls

entry_points.name: process-launch
entry_points.description: Application launch creates the shell window and composition root

entry_points.name: window-startup
entry_points.description: MainWindow builds the visual tree, initializes the playback host, and subscribes to workflow and shell projections

entry_points.name: open-file-command
entry_points.description: Open file UI action enters through MainWindow and forwards to shell/workflow services

entry_points.name: backend-media-opened
entry_points.description: Backend media-open event flows through the host adapter into shell resume/open logic

entry_points.name: backend-media-ended
entry_points.description: Backend media-ended event flows through the host adapter into auto-advance and resume cleanup logic

steps.description: ShellController loads the playlist item and applies playback defaults
MpvPlaybackBackend forwards media load to MpvPlaybackEngine
MpvPlaybackEngine publishes normalized playback state and track changes
PlaybackBackendCoordinator applies backend state, tracks, and clock updates to MediaSessionCoordinator
MediaSessionCoordinator updates the timeline, streams, and active subtitle presentation state
ShellProjectionService converts MediaSession into immutable UI projections consumed by MainWindow
SubtitleApplicationService decides sidecar, embedded, cached-generated, or generated-caption flow for the current media
DefaultSubtitleSourceResolver imports external or embedded subtitle cues
DefaultCaptionGenerator resolves available transcription providers and generates subtitle cues
SubtitleApplicationService builds stable transcript and translation segments with provenance and writes them through MediaSessionCoordinator
Session-driven translation trigger watches the active transcript segment and translates only when a matching translated segment is missing
SubtitleWorkflowProjectionAdapter and SubtitlePresentationProjector turn workflow state plus MediaSession state into UI and presenter-ready projections

video_steps.description: MainWindow hosts PlaybackHostAdapter.View in the player pane
PlaybackHostAdapter initializes MpvVideoPresenter with the playback backend
MpvHostControl creates the native host window and binds the playback backend to it
MpvPlaybackEngine launches mpv with the host window id and named-pipe IPC

subtitle_steps.description: MainWindow builds a SubtitlePresentationModel and delegates to StageCoordinator
StageCoordinator computes stage-relative placement and delegates rendering to ISubtitlePresenter
DetachedWindowSubtitlePresenter renders the model in SubtitleOverlayWindow

native_interop_points.name: mpv-child-window-hosting
native_interop_points.mechanism: Win32 child window creation and message subclassing
native_interop_points.details: Creates a native child window, subclasses its window proc, syncs bounds with WinUI layout, and forwards input/fullscreen/shortcut activity

native_interop_points.name: mpv-process-ipc
native_interop_points.details: Launches mpv.exe with --wid and --input-ipc-server, then reads/writes JSON messages through NamedPipeClientStream

native_interop_points.name: subtitle-overlay-windowing
native_interop_points.mechanism: Detached owned window with explicit AppWindow positioning
native_interop_points.details: Creates a transparent overlay window, parents it to the owner HWND, and positions it over the video stage

native_interop_points.name: fullscreen-overlay-windowing
native_interop_points.mechanism: Detached owned window with explicit AppWindow positioning and pointer state tracking
native_interop_points.details: Hosts the fullscreen control overlay separately from the main window

native_interop_points.name: window-mode-control
native_interop_points.mechanism: Windows App SDK AppWindow presenter switching
native_interop_points.details: Switches between standard, borderless, compact overlay, and fullscreen presenters

native_interop_points.name: runtime-download-bootstrap
native_interop_points.mechanism: Http download plus archive extraction into app data
native_interop_points.details: Bootstraps mpv, ffmpeg, and llama.cpp at runtime

likely_refactor_targets.why: Still the largest shell integration class with code-built UI, event wiring, projection application, and multiple UI behavior paths
Manual composition root is correct architecturally but now carries a long, infrastructure-heavy object graph factory
Workflow split is complete at the public boundary, but orchestration, caching, translation control, runtime coordination, and status choreography remain concentrated here
Still exposes a legacy transport/control surface even though the architecture now routes playback control through IPlaybackBackend and ShellController
Infrastructure hotspot for process lifecycle, named-pipe IPC, property observation, and mpv-specific state parsing
Composition factory, registries, availability logic, and runtime path resolution still live in one file
